summary: Parallel review: FAIL (2 critical, 5 warning, 11 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 test/worktree.test.mjs:367 — `Promise.all` over synchronous `execFileSync` runs serially in one event loop; true cross-process race on `.team/worktrees/` is not exercised. Add a backlog item for a real two-process spawn test.

🟡 test/worktree.test.mjs:375 — `Promise.all` over `execFileSync` (sync) is effectively serial on the event loop; not a true OS-level race. Assertions still hold but framing oversells. Backlog: spawn child processes for genuine concurrency.
🟡 test/worktree.test.mjs:370 — No coverage for two concurrent invocations on the **same** slug (the actual race case where both see `existsSync === false`).

🟡 bin/lib/run.mjs:164 — `worktreePath` uses raw `slug` while branch uses `slugToBranch(slug)`; normalize/validate the slug before joining into the on-disk path to close a latent path-traversal via `--slug ../foo`.
No findings in the four 🔴 veto categories (dead code, premature abstraction, unnecessary indirection, gold-plating).

🟡 test/worktree.test.mjs:375 — `Promise.all` over a synchronous `execFileSync`-based function is microtask-ordered, not truly parallel; either fork/worker_threads or soften the "Race them in parallel" wording.
